items.name: Lance
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Lance – image/video generation and understanding in one model
items.summary: Image/video model that commenters are already fork-trying for UI understanding.
items.why_included: Strong comment praise plus a concrete UI/UX use case.
items.evidence: Commenters said video understanding could help with UI/UX navigation, and one said they had forked it to try.

items.name: Stable Audio 3
items.summary: Audio generation service that commenters praised for speed and smaller models.
items.why_included: High-signal praise and a tangible performance note.
items.evidence: Commenters called it a great release, praised the smaller models and licensing, and reported sub-2-second generation for 120 seconds of audio.

items.name: Qwen3.7-Max
items.summary: Qwen frontier model release that commenters compared favorably to top rivals.
items.why_included: Strong model-thread signal with direct positive comparisons.
items.evidence: Commenters compared the Qwen family favorably to Opus, Gemini, and GPT, and recommended dense models for hard tasks.

items.name: OpenCode
items.summary: Open-source Claude Code alternative for agentic coding.
items.why_included: Explicitly recommended as a practical free alternative.
items.evidence: A commenter said Qwen3.6 with llama.cpp and OpenCode was a great free alternative to Claude Code.

items.name: Flipper One
items.summary: Pocket hacker gadget with commenters liking the bigger Swiss-army-knife scope.
items.why_included: Concrete hardware with clear curiosity and praise.
items.evidence: Commenters said it is more than an incremental Flipper Zero upgrade and liked the Swiss-army-knife angle.

items.name: VitePlus
items.summary: Build-time tool for Node.js developers, recommended by name in comments.
items.why_included: Direct recommendation in a relevant dev-thread.
items.evidence: A commenter told Node.js build-time users to check it out and called VoidZero's tools consistently excellent.

items.name: tea
items.source_story_title: Handling the great code forge fragmentation
items.summary: Gitea CLI for repo and issue work, pitched as a gh alternative for agents.
items.why_included: Clear agent-workflow endorsement from a real user.
items.evidence: A commenter said they had success with Gitea and using tea as a gh-cli alternative for agents.

items.name: Liquid Haskell
items.source_story_title: Formal Verification Gates for AI Coding Loops
items.summary: Refinement-types tool for encoding invariants directly in Haskell.
items.why_included: Strong alternative to sidecar verification and codegen.
items.evidence: A commenter suggested Liquid Haskell or Lean to put invariants directly in the program instead of a sidecar.

items.name: Modal token timing simulator
items.source_story_title: How fast is N tokens per second really?
items.summary: Token-timing visualizer that makes LLM throughput feel tangible.
items.why_included: Useful for sanity-checking speed claims and local-model tradeoffs.
items.evidence: A commenter linked their own similar simulator and liked using real model output to render token timing.
